messages.content:
Thank you for your email. 

We regret to inform you that Gigi will not be available during the proposed time slot (i.e. tomorrow at 5:30pm). 

Please note that Gigi will be available this Wednesday afternoon. Please let us know if you will be available and your preferred time slot. 

Otherwise, we will need to re-schedule your HK tax briefing to next week and please provide your availability for our further arrangement. 



check grammar

Thank you for your email.

We regret to inform you that Gigi will not be available during the proposed time slot (tomorrow at 5:30pm).

Please note that Gigi will be available this Wednesday afternoon. Kindly let us know if you will be available and your preferred time slot.

Otherwise, we will need to reschedule your HK tax briefing to next week. Please provide your availability for further arrangements.

Kind regards,

Please note that Gigi will be available this Wednesday afternoon. Kindly let us know if you will be available and your preferred time slot. Please also provide your availability for next week and we will see how we can fit into your  schedule. 

check grammar

Please note that Gigi will be available this Wednesday afternoon. Kindly inform us if you will be available and provide your preferred time slot. Additionally, please provide your availability for next week and we will do our best to accommodate your schedule.

Thank you.
rephrase

Gigi will be able to meet on Wednesday afternoon. Please let us know your availability and preferred time slot. If you are unavailable, kindly give us your schedule for next week so we may arrange accordingly.

Thank you.

Please also provide your availability for next week and we will see how we can fit into your  schedule. 
sentence ok?
Yes, the sentence is grammatically correct.

This is a gentle reminder for our below email. 

We would like to schedule a HK tax briefing with you further to the below email. Please could you provide us with 3 available timeslots in the coming 2 weeks so we can further discuss the HK tax implications in relation to end of your Short Term Assignment to Hong Kong? Thanks. 


check grammar

This is a gentle reminder for the email below.

We would like to arrange a HK tax briefing with you in relation to the end of your Short Term Assignment to Hong Kong. Kindly provide us with three available time slots within the next two weeks so that we can arrange a suitable time to discuss HK tax implications.

Thank you.

We would like to follow up again on our below emails. 
check grammar

The sentence is grammatically correct. However, it could be more specific if it is followed by the email that needs to be followed up on. 

For example: 

We would like to follow up again on our previous email regarding the HK tax briefing. We have yet to receive a response from you. 

Thank you.
not a tax briefing , but to follow up on requested information

In that case, you could rephrase it as follows:

We would like to follow up again on the requested information that was mentioned in our previous email. We have not yet received the information and would appreciate it if you could provide us with an update.

Thank you.

Further to below email, please note that we have yet to receive any respond from the below assignees. 

As you are the Host RM for the below assignees, please could you assist in reminding them to respond to Deloitte HK team's email? Many thanks.




check grammar

Below is a suggested alternative that is grammatically correct:

Further to the email below, we would like to inform you that we have not received a response from the assignees mentioned in the email.

As you are the Host RM for these assignees, would you kindly remind them to respond to Deloitte HK team's email? We appreciate your assistance in this matter.

Thank you.

Hope you are well.

Please could you provide us the following information in order for us to assist with your HK tax filings:-


check grammar

The sentence is grammatically correct, but it is missing information about what specific information is being requested. 

A suggested revision would be:

I hope this email finds you well. In order for us to assist with your HK tax filings, could you please provide us with the following information:

[Insert specific information being requested]

Thank you.
rephrase

Here is a rephrased version of the given email:

I hope this email finds you in good health. Our team requires certain information from you to facilitate your HK tax filings. May we request you to kindly provide us with the information mentioned below:

[Insert the information required]

We appreciate your cooperation, and please don't hesitate to contact us in case you have any concerns.

Thank you.
